original_hypothesis.title: GaugeController unbounded state growth via add_type may permanently DoS checkpointing and weight writes
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: GaugeController._get_total loops over all gauge types each time, calling _get_sum and _get_type_weight for each type. Governance can add arbitrary many types via add_type, increasing the per-call runtime of _get_total (and therefore checkpoint, checkpoint_gauge, gauge_relative_weight_write, and LendingLedger.update_market which calls gauge_relative_weight_write). Excessive gauge types can make these calls exceed gas limits, bricking reward updates and claims.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Even if governance is trusted, misconfiguration or compromised governance can irreversibly harm protocol operations. The code has an O(numTypes) loop inside frequently called write paths, making it fragile to state growth.
deployment_assumptions: Governance adds a very large number of gauge types over time

proof_of_signal.lines: _get_total(): loops over n_gauge_types and then loops up to 500 weeks
proof_of_signal.pattern: nested loops with governance-controlled n_gauge_types growth

preconditions: n_gauge_types becomes large enough that _get_total gas exceeds block gas limits
Core flows depend on _get_total (directly or via gauge_relative_weight_write from LendingLedger)

initial_code_evidence.vulnerable_pattern: O(n) type iteration in commonly-invoked write paths
initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: reward updates and claims become uncallable as types grow
initial_code_evidence.missing_safeguards: no cap on n_gauge_types; no batching mechanism; no per-type lazy checkpoint in _get_total caller paths

analysis_questions: What is the worst-case gas of _get_total for realistic weeks missed and n_gauge_types?
Are there alternative call paths in LendingLedger that avoid gauge_relative_weight_write under stress?
Is n_gauge_types expected to remain small by design (and enforced off-chain)?
validation_requirements: Gas benchmark _get_total with varying n_gauge_types and missed weeks
Simulate production usage where multiple markets call update_market frequently

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: After many add_type calls, any call to checkpoint or gauge_relative_weight_write becomes too expensive. LendingLedger.update_market fails, blocking sync_ledger and claim. Protocol reward distribution halts until redeploy/migration.
prerequisites: Governance misuse/compromise OR accidental type spam

potential_impact_if_confirmed.financial_impact: Reward distribution and accounting can halt; users cannot reliably claim; downstream transfers that call sync_ledger may revert indirectly
potential_impact_if_confirmed.affected_users: All markets using gauge_relative_weight_write and all reward claimants
potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: May require redeployment or administrative cleanup that may be impossible if core write paths are bricked

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: No gas growth/regression tests covering large n_gauge_types.

technical_investigation_focus.gas_analysis_needed: yes (worst-case loops: 500 weeks * n_gauge_types + inner operations)
technical_investigation_focus.execution_timing: multi-year accumulation or rapid governance actions
technical_investigation_focus.detection_analysis: gas usage trending upward; eventual out-of-gas reverts

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
